result:
Done. I implemented the three missing operations in `CsvRow.cs`, and the project builds cleanly with no warnings.

- `EscapeField` returns the field verbatim unless `NeedsQuoting` holds, in which case it doubles internal quotes and wraps the result in double-quotes.
- `JoinRow` escapes each field and joins with commas; the empty list yields the empty string.
- `SplitRow` walks the line field by field: a field starting with `"` is scanned to its closing un-doubled quote (doubled quotes become one literal quote, commas/CR/LF inside are content); otherwise the field runs to the next comma or end of line. The empty line yields one empty field, and a trailing comma yields a trailing empty field.

I verified the behavior end-to-end with a throwaway console app outside the repo (since test files aren't allowed in the project): round-trips of plain, empty, comma-containing, quote-containing, and CR/LF-containing fields all reproduce the input exactly, including the edge cases the spec calls out (`JoinRow([])` → `""`, which parses back as a single empty field; trailing commas; fields that are only quotes). The scratch directory was removed afterward, and `NeedsQuoting` is unchanged.
